item.title: U.S. disables ship for allegedly violating Iran blockade amid Strait of Hormuz feud
item.description: For the first time since President Trump reinstated a blockade on Iranian ports, the U.S. military has disabled a ship that allegedly tried to sail to Kharg Island.

item.title: Argentina beats England to reach FIFA World Cup final
item.description: Major League Soccer analyst Kacey White joins "The Takeout" to break down Argentina's last-minute semifinal win against England and what to expect from the World Cup final.

item.title: 103 House Democrats vote to cut off aid to Israel
item.description: More than 100 House Democrats vote to cut off aid to Israel on Wednesday, the latest flashpoint over support for the U.S. ally. Kendra Barkoff Lamy, former press secretary to then-Vice President Joe Biden, and Marc Short, former Chief of Staff for Vice President Mike Pence, join "The Takeout" to discuss

item.title: Maine congressional candidate Matt Dunlap on his campaign, Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democrat Matt Dunlap joins "The Takeout" to discuss his race for U.S. House and address Graham Platner's controversial Senate campaign.

item.title: GPS data tracks boat Nolan Wells was on before he went missing
item.description: GPS data from the Mississippi Department of Marine Resources, obtained by CBS News, tracks the movements of the boat that carried Nolan Wells to Horn Island on July 4.

item.title: Pentagon proposes new secrecy power to withhold unclassified records from FOIA
item.description: The legislative proposal would allow the defense secretary to withhold "controlled unclassified information," potentially curbing public access to a wide range of defense records.

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes for fifth consecutive day as mutual threats intensify
item.description: The U.S. and Iran continued trading strikes on Wednesday as President Trump threatened to start hitting civilian infrastructure. CBS News' national security contributor Samantha Vinograd joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Argentina rallies past bitter rival England to book spot in World Cup final
item.description: Defending World Cup champion Argentina rallied to edge England 2-1 to advance to Sunday's final at MetLife Stadium, where they will face Spain.

item.title: Trump to allege Chinese meddling in U.S. elections in primetime speech, sources say
item.description: Part of President Trump's speech Thursday night is expected to touch on previously unreported alleged Chinese meddling in U.S. elections, according to sources familiar with the matter.

item.title: Trump overturns ICE order that suspended most traffic stops
item.description: President Trump has reversed a decision to suspend most ICE traffic stops following two deadly shootings that garnered national attention. CBS News' Nicole Sganga reports and Mark Morgan, who served as acting ICE director during the first Trump administration, joins "The Takeout" to offer his take.

item.title: New GPS data for boat Nolan Wells was on before disappearance
item.description: CBS News has obtained GPS data showing the movements of the boat that carried Nolan Wells before he disappeared on the Fourth of July. Wells was found dead on July 6. CBS News' Anna Schecter has more.

item.title: Samsung set to unveil new foldable Galaxy phones, with durability as its ultimate test
item.description: Tech giant Samsung is set to debut a new line of foldable phones at its Galaxy Unpacked event next week. CNET senior technology reporter Abrar Al-Heeti joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Inside the questioning at Blanche and Clayton's confirmation hearings
item.description: Todd Blanche and Jay Clayton had respective confirmation hearings Wednesday for attorney general and director of national intelligence. Major Garrett takes a look at some of the questions they faced before the Senate.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Blanche acknowledges Epstein files redaction "mistakes"
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche acknowledged redaction errors in the release of the Epstein files as he faced intense questioning during his Senate confirmation hearing on Wednesday. CBS News' Jake Rosen reports.

item.title: Details on New York's data center construction pause, a first in the U.S.
item.description: New York has become the first state in the U.S. to impose a moratorium on the construction of massive AI data centers. The pause takes effect immediately and halts the construction of new data centers for up to a year. Paris Marx, a technology podcast host, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: House vote to end Israel aid divides Democrats, with 103 voting in favor
item.description: The amendment, which had no chance of passing, put Democrats in a politically tough spot

item.title: England-Argentina World Cup semifinal reignites painful historic rivalry
item.description: The World Cup semifinal between England and Argentina was a grudge match, reopening decades-old wounds for both countries.

item.title: 1 dead, 3 missing after boat capsizes near San Francisco's Alcatraz Island
item.description: A pontoon boat capsized near San Francisco's Alcatraz Island on Tuesday, triggering a dramatic search operation. Authorities say one person died and three others are still missing. CBS News Bay Area reporter Madeline White has more.

item.title: Hegseth requires testosterone deficiency screening for service members over 30
item.description: The Pentagon will now require service members over 30 to screen for testosterone deficiency, and if needed, they can elect to have testosterone replacement therapy.

item.title: Houston prosecutor could bring charges against ICE agents in fatal shooting
item.description: Harris County District Attorney Sean Teare, who is investigating the fatal ICE shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, told CBS News ICE's tactics "in no way resemble" the behavior of police agencies he's worked with.

item.title: U.S. launches back-to-back waves of strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. launched two waves of strikes against Iran on Wednesday, marking the fifth day of attacks against the country as the war ramps back up. CBS News' Aaron Navarro reports.

item.title: Wildfires expose millions in Midwest, Northeast to dangerous smoke
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Senior defense officials looking at Cuba military options
item.description: Military planners have examined options for possible action against Cuba, including an Army-led air assault involving the 101st Airborne Division.

item.title: Here's what the Common Cents Act means for businesses, consumers
item.description: New law aims to address how businesses and consumers should transact amid the phase-out of the penny.

item.title: Cyclospora outbreak leaves consumers guessing which foods are safe
item.description: Officials are still searching for the source of the outbreak, prompting consumers to seek advice on social media about which foods to avoid.

item.title: U.S. Mint to make $1 gold coin with Trump's face on it
item.description: It's the latest effort by President Trump to make his mark on Washington, D.C.

item.title: Alzheimer's blood test may show who's likely to develop it in 5 to 10 years
item.description: A blood test may predict if apparently healthy older adults are likely to develop Alzheimer's symptoms in the next five or 10 years, researchers say.

item.title: AI companies are creating "generative ghosts" of deceased loved ones
item.description: Startups are using emails, photos and voice recordings to create AI simulations that family and friends can interact with after a loved one's death.

item.title: Have a Capital One credit card? Take these 5 steps
item.description: If you're a Capital One customer, consider adding extra levels of security to keep scammers from misusing your data
